chunk_summary: This section outlines minimum cybersecurity baseline controls for pediatric tele-nutrition platforms in India, emphasizing data security, device hygiene, operational resilience, and vendor assurance to protect sensitive child health data.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of cybersecurity in pediatric tele-nutrition platforms.
Identify key baseline controls for data security, device hygiene, and operational resilience.
Learn how to implement vendor assurance and staff training to mitigate cybersecurity risks.
key_facts: Tele-nutrition platforms handle high-risk child data, including growth metrics, photos, and lab reports.
Minimum controls include multi-factor authentication, role-based access, and encryption (TLS) for data in transit and at rest.
Device hygiene requires screen locks, encrypted devices, and rapid account deactivation for lost phones.
Operational resilience includes maintaining backups, downtime workflows, and periodic restoration tests.
Vendor assurance mandates documented security controls, breach notification clauses, and data export capabilities.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ‘Identity and access: unique user accounts, strong passwords with multi-factor authentication where feasible, and role-based access (coordinator versus dietitian versus RMP).’
